Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Notable findings include mildly low fasting glucose, which may warrant monitoring for hypoglycemia, and a slightly elevated LDL cholesterol, suggesting increased cardiovascular risk. Renal function, electrolytes, and other metabolic parameters are normal. No evidence of microalbuminuria. Overall, results are stable with minor concerns regarding glucose and lipid management.
